page: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a single dedicated physical core and vCPU utilizing the x86_64 architecture, specifically an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is average, while read bandwidth is weak. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly, but complex multi-threaded tasks, ray tracing, and image processing show poor performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7a.medium offers dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources at a low entry point, making it suitable for single-threaded workloads that benefit from high clock speeds without requiring massive memory footprints. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage, limited multi-core scaling, and low baseline network throughput. This instance is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and small-scale single-threaded utility scripts.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring a single dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 vCPU running at 3.7 GHz and 2.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers strong single-core performance, as evidenced by top-25% scores in single-core stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks. However, its single-core, single-threaded architecture limits multi-core scaling, resulting in lower performance for parallelized tasks, database operations, and heavy image processing. The server lacks local storage and bundled GPUs, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. It is qualitatively cost-effective for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic text processing, and utility scripts that require dedicated CPU execution without high memory or network demands.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring a single dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC core, 2.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.medium is a compute-optimized server with 1 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU, 2.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A single-core, compute-optimized virtual server featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC processor and high-speed DDR5 memory for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 core running at 3.7 GHz
2.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight, single-threaded utility workloads
